description: Enterprise Application (Service Principal) Holds a Strong Role on a Subscription
rationale: An Enterprise Application (Service Principal) that holds a strong Azure RBAC role (Owner, Contributor, User Access Administrator, or a custom role granting similarly broad/wildcard actions) directly at subscription scope can control or take over resources - and potentially other identities - within that subscription. Anyone who can add credentials to, or is an owner of, that Service Principal's application effectively inherits this subscription-level power. Review who owns/manages each flagged application and whether the assigned role is actually needed.
remediation: Apply least privilege: replace broad built-in roles (Owner/Contributor/User Access Administrator) assigned to service principals with narrower built-in or custom roles scoped to only the actions and resources the application needs. Restrict who can own or manage credentials for applications holding subscription-level roles, and monitor/rotate their credentials regularly.
dashboard_name: Enterprise Applications With Strong Subscription Roles
